title: AI Playbook Eval Rubric
description: Schema for evals/rubrics/<agent>.json. Source of truth for rubric shape; loaded by tests/acceptance/test_eval_contracts.py and by evals/run_eval.py at validate-samples time.

agent.description: Agent id — kebab-case, must match the filename stem and the agents/<id>.agent.md frontmatter id.

version.description: Schema version. Bump only on breaking rubric-shape changes.

must_demonstrate.description: Behaviours the agent's output must exhibit.

must_not.description: Anti-patterns the agent's output must avoid.

quality_signals.description: Soft signals the LLM judge uses to assess quality.

id.description: Stable human-readable id like `RELEASE-MUST-001` (must_demonstrate), `XP-NOT-005` (must_not), or `STORY-QUALITY-002` (quality_signals). The middle token is fixed: MUST | NOT | QUALITY. Used by the Stay-rule coverage map; renaming an id is a contract break.

criterion.description: Plain-English statement of the behaviour. The LLM judge sees this verbatim.

keywords.description: Tokens that cheap structural validation greps for before invoking the judge.

evidence.description: Optional concrete example the judge can anchor on. Recommended for must_demonstrate; less useful for must_not.
